title: Dna-zyme based methods & compositions for treating huntington's disease
abstract: Provided herein are methods and compositions for treating Huntington's disease. For example, provided are compositions that include DNA oligonucleotides that have targeting specificity for RNA encoding mutant huntingtin protein. When introduced into a cell, the DNA oligonucleotides reduce expression of the mutant huntingtin protein in a cell. For example, the DNA oligonucleotide, functioning as an enzymatic DNA molecule (or DNAzyme), cleaves the RNA encoding the mutant mRNA protein, thereby rendering the mRNA incapable of expression. That is, the cleavage event renders the RNA nonÂ¬ functional and reduces or abrogates protein expression from that RNA. Hence, synthesis of the mutant huntingtin protein is selectively reduced or inhibited, in accordance with the methods and compositions described herein. By silencing or reducing the expression of the mutant huntingtin protein, the methods and compositions describe herein can be used to treat Huntington's disease, including adult and juvenile onset Huntington's disease.
claims: Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1. A composition comprising a DNA oligonucleotide having binding specificity for a target region of a messenger ribonucleotide (mRNA) encoding a mutant huntingtin protein. 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide is a catalytic antisense polynucleotide. 3. The composition of claim 1 or 2, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide is a DNAzyme. 4. The composition of any of claims 1-3, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide comprises a nucleotide sequence that is at least 95% identical to any one of SEQ ID NOS: 1-4. 5. The composition of any of claims 1-4, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide comprises a modification that increases the stability of the DNA oligonucleotide. 6. The composition of claim 5, wherein the modification comprises an inverted deoxythymidine at the 3' end of the DNA oligonucleotide. 7. The composition of any of claims 1-6, wherein the target region of the mRNA comprises a mutant huntingtin exon 1 mRNA sequence. 8. A method of modulating cellular expression of mutant huntingtin protein, the method comprising contacting a cell with at least one DNA oligonucleotide having binding specificity for a target region of a messenger ribonucleotide (mRNA) encoding the mutant huntingtin protein. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide comprises a nucleotide sequence that is at least 95% identical to any one of SEQ ID NOS: 1-4. 10. The method of claim 8 or 9, wherein modulating cellular expression of the mutant huntingtin protein comprises inhibiting expression of the mutant huntingtin protein. 11. The method of any of claims 8-10, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide is a catalytic antisense polynucleotide. 12. The method of claim any of claims 8-11, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide is a DNAzyme. 13. The method of any of claims 8-12, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide comprises a modification that increases the stability of the DNA oligonucleotide. 14. The method of any of claims 8-13, wherein contacting the cell with the DNA oligonucleotide results in introduction of the DNA oligonucleotide into the contacted cell. 15. The method of any of claims 8-14, wherein contacting the cell with at least one DNA oligonucleotide results in a reduction of mutant huntingtin protein. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the mutant huntingtin protein is reduced by at least 20%, 25%, 30%, 35%, or 40%. 17. A method of treating Huntington's disease in a subject, the method comprising administering to the subject at least one DNA oligonucleotide having binding specificity for a target region of a messenger ribonucleotide (mRNA) encoding a mutant huntingtin protein. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide comprises a nucleotide sequence that is at least 95% identical to any one of SEQ ID NOS: 1-4. 19. The method of claim 17 or 18, wherein the method further comprises reducing expression of the mutant huntingtin protein in the subject. 20. The method of any of claims 17-19, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide is a catalytic antisense polynucleotide. 21. The method of claim any of claims 17-20, wherein the DNA oligonucleotide is a DNAzyme. 22. The method of claim any of claims 17-21, further comprising selecting a subject. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein selecting the subject comprises testing the subject to determine whether the subject has Huntington's disease. 24. The method of any of claims 17-23, wherein the target region of the mRNA comprises a mutant huntingtin exon 1 mRNA. 25. The method of any of claims 17-24, wherein administering to the subject at least one DNA oligonucleotide results in reduction of the mutant huntingtin protein. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the mutant huntingtin protein is reduced by at least 20%, 25%, 30%, 35%, or 40%. 27. An enzymatic DNA molecule comprising a polynucleotide sequence having binding specificity for a target region of a messenger ribonucleotide (mRNA) encoding a mutant huntingtin protein. 28. The enzymatic DNA molecule of claim 27, wherein the DNA molecule is a catalytic antisense polynucleotide. 29. The enzymatic DNA molecule of claim any of claims 27-28, wherein the polynucleotide sequence comprises a nucleotide sequence that is at least 95% identical to any one of SEQ ID NOS: 1-4. 30. The enzymatic DNA molecule of any of claims 27-29, wherein the DNA molecule comprises a modification that increases the stability of the DNA molecule. 31. The enzymatic DNA molecule of claim 30, wherein the modification comprises an inverted deoxythymidine at the 3' end of the DNA molecule. 32. The enzymatic DNA molecule of claim 31, wherein the target region of the mRNA comprises a mutant huntingtin exon 1 mRNA. 33. The enzymatic DNA molecule of any of claims 27-32, wherein the enzymatic DNA molecule comprises a catalytic domain that is at least 95% identical to the nucleic acid sequence set forth as SEQ ID NO: 7. 34. The enzymatic DNA molecule of claim 33, wherein the catalytic domain is flanked on both sides by a substrate binding domain. 35. The enzymatic DNA molecule of any of claims 27-34, wherein administration of the enzymatic DNA molecule to a subject reduces expression of mutant huntingtin protein in the subject by at least 20%, 25%, 30%, 35%, or 40%.
